get.summary: Get all campaigns for an ad account

post.summary: Create a campaign for an ad account

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Unique key for retrying this create request. If a matching prior create is visible, the existing object is returned instead of creating a duplicate. Must contain at least one non-whitespace character and be at most 255 characters.

get.summary: List custom audiences for the authenticated ad account.

post.summary: Create a custom audience for the authenticated ad account.

get.summary: Get a custom audience for the authenticated ad account.

post.summary: Create a custom audience from an uploaded file and start processing.
Archive a custom audience for the authenticated ad account.

get.summary: Get metadata for the ad account
Get ad account insights aggregated by time granularity.

parameters.name: time_granularity
parameters.description: Time bucket size. `none` returns one bucket for the requested time range. Segmented requests support `none`, `daily`, and `monthly`.

schema.description: Time bucket size. `none` returns one bucket for the requested time range. Segmented requests support `none`, `daily`, and `monthly`.

parameters.name: aggregation_level
parameters.description: Entity grain inside the endpoint scope. Changing it from `campaign` to `ad` changes rows from campaign totals to ad totals; it does not widen the endpoint scope.

schema.description: Entity grain inside the endpoint scope. Changing it from `campaign` to `ad` changes rows from campaign totals to ad totals; it does not widen the endpoint scope.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of rows to return. Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: time_ranges
parameters.description: Report window filter. Send one JSON-encoded `unix_range` object with Unix timestamp `start` and `end` values in seconds, aligned to full-hour boundaries in the ad account timezone.

schema.description: Report window filter. Send one JSON-encoded `unix_range` object with Unix timestamp `start` and `end` values in seconds, aligned to full-hour boundaries in the ad account timezone.

parameters.name: filters
parameters.description: Row filters encoded as JSON strings. Entity filters use `IN`; numeric metadata and grouped metric filters use `GREATER_THAN` or `LESS_THAN`. Multiple filters are ANDed together.

schema.description: Row filters encoded as JSON strings. Entity filters use `IN`; numeric metadata and grouped metric filters use `GREATER_THAN` or `LESS_THAN`. Multiple filters are ANDed together.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Additional row keys to return. Every row includes `id`, `start_time`, and `end_time`; `fields[]` adds metrics, IDs, names, and metadata. Use canonical names such as `campaign.id`, `metadata.readable_time`, `product.feed_id`, `product.item_id`, `country.name`, and `device.type`.

schema.description: Additional row keys to return. Every row includes `id`, `start_time`, and `end_time`; `fields[]` adds metrics, IDs, names, and metadata. Use canonical names such as `campaign.id`, `metadata.readable_time`, `product.feed_id`, `product.item_id`, `country.name`, and `device.type`.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Row ordering encoded as JSON strings with `field` and `direction`. Applied before `limit` and pagination. Directions are `asc` and `desc`.

schema.description: Row ordering encoded as JSON strings with `field` and `direction`. Applied before `limit` and pagination. Directions are `asc` and `desc`.

parameters.name: segments
parameters.description: Optional extra group-by dimension for enabled ad accounts. Supported values are `product`, `country`, and `device`; at most one segment is supported. `product` requires `fields[]` to include `product.feed_id` or `product.item_id`.

schema.description: Optional extra group-by dimension for enabled ad accounts. Supported values are `product`, `country`, and `device`; at most one segment is supported. `product` requires `fields[]` to include `product.feed_id` or `product.item_id`.

parameters.name: override_segment_group_order
parameters.description: Order of the aggregation level and requested segment. Include each exactly once.

schema.description: Order of the aggregation level and requested segment. Include each exactly once.

parameters.name: includes
parameters.description: Optional result expansion. `zero_impression_products` is available only for enabled ad accounts and requires `segments[]=product` and `product` first in `override_segment_group_order[]`.

schema.description: Optional result expansion. `zero_impression_products` is available only for enabled ad accounts and requires `segments[]=product` and `product` first in `override_segment_group_order[]`.
